title: agropyron repens f. subpubescens
intro: Agropyron repens f. subpubescens, also known as a subspecies of the creeping bentgrass or black grass, is a type of perennial grass that belongs to the wheat grass family (Poaceae). This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, as well as its ability to reduce fever and alleviate respiratory issues. The health benefits of Agropyron repens f. subpubescens include its potential to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and its dried leaves and roots can also be used in herbal remedies and traditional medicine practices.
